seoTitle: How to Add Video to Webflow (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Add native video elements or background video to your Webflow site, from uploading files to linking YouTube or Vimeo, without breaking your layout.
intro: Video can make a Webflow page feel alive, but it needs to be added carefully to avoid slow load times and broken layouts. This guide covers adding native video elements and background video in Webflow, including linking external platforms and keeping things responsive.

steps.heading: Add a video element
steps.body: Open the Add Elements panel and drag a native video component or background video layer onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Link an external video or upload a file
steps.body: Paste a YouTube or Vimeo URL into the element settings, or use the asset manager to upload a compressed MP4 file for background loops.

steps.heading: Configure playback settings
steps.body: Toggle autoplay, loop, and muted playback options as needed, keeping in mind that autoplay usually requires the video to be muted.

steps.heading: Improve readability with overlays
steps.body: Add a semi-transparent color overlay over background videos so any text placed on top stays readable.

steps.heading: Test responsiveness and publish
steps.body: Check how the video behaves across tablet and mobile breakpoints, adjust padding as needed, then publish and verify load performance.

faq.question: Do I need to mute a video for autoplay to work?
faq.answer: Yes, most browsers require background videos to be muted for autoplay to function correctly.

faq.question: Can I embed a YouTube or Vimeo video instead of uploading a file?
faq.answer: Yes, you can paste an external URL from platforms like YouTube or Vimeo directly into the video element's settings.

faq.question: Will a background video slow down my page?
faq.answer: It can if the file isn't compressed properly, so keep background videos short and optimized for web use.

faq.question: How do I keep text readable over a video background?
faq.answer: Add a semi-transparent overlay layer between the video and your text to maintain contrast.
